LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUN15-2023 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF FREDERICK LEWIS' REQUEST TO SET HEARING ON MOTION FOR RECLASSIFICATION, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S EQUEST GRANTED. COURT SUA SPONTE VACATES THE 6/1/23 ORDER. MOTION FOR RECLASSIFICATION WILL NOW BE HEARD ON 6/29/23. ANY REPLY TO PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION SHALL BE FILED/SERVED IN CONFORMANCE WITH CCP 1005. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TIFFANY R. NORMAN AND KENNETH J. SPERANDIO, JR.'S MOTION FOR RECLASSIFICATION. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS THE FOLLOWING TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS TIFFANY R. NORMAN AND KENNETH J. SPERANDIO, JR.'S MOTION FOR RECLASSIFICATION IS GRANTED. AMOUNTS IN DISPUTE ARE ABOVE THE JURISDICTIONAL LIMIT. (CCP 403.040.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B, ULMER JR., CLERK: R. WOODS, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR RECLASSIFICATION
Off Calendar Jun-29-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: DEFENDANTS TIFFANY R. NORMAN AND KENNETH J. SPERANDIO, JR.'S MOTION FOR RECLASSIFICATION.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The court adopts the following tentative ruling:

Defendants TIFFANY R. NORMAN AND KENNETH J. SPERANDIO, JR.'S motion for reclassification is granted. Amounts in dispute are above the jurisdictional limit. (CCP 403.040.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Request To Set Hearing On Motion For Reclassification
Off Calendar Jun-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF FREDERICK LEWIS' Request To Set Hearing On Motion For Reclassification.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The request to set a hearing on the motion for reclassification is granted. Pursuant to CCP 128(a)(8), the court sua sponte vacates the 6/1/23 order granting the motion for reclassification. The court erroneously granted that motion on the original hearing date of 4/11/23 as unopposed even though the parties agreed that the hearing should occur on 4/28/23. (joint stipulation filed 3/30/23.) The court was unaware of the contemplated continuance when it issued its 4/11/23 ruling. The motion for reclassification will now be heard on 6/29/23, and any reply to plaintiff's opposition to the motion for reclassification shall be filed and served in conformance with CCP 1005.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
